data.title: Gas Station Operator EG Group Motors Towards $9B U.S. IPO
data.description:
Gas station operator EG Group is set for a $9 billion IPO in the U.S. by the summer.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Aiming to Raise $1B

According to a report in the Financial Times, the company founded by brothers Mohsin and Zuber Issa and backed by TDR Capital will look to raise about $1 billion when it lists in the first half of 2026. That will value the group at around $9 billion.

It is understood that banks including Bank of America (BAC) and Goldman Sachs (GS) are advising on the IPO. The listing is expected to take place under the Cumberland Farms name – the US forecourt retail operator EG owns in the U.S.

The gas forecourts giant founded in Blackburn, England, in 2001 offers Grocery & Merchandise, Foodservice, and Fuel services.

It has sites in 8 countries, including the U.S., and revenues of just over $24 billion. According to its website, it serves 2.5 million customers every day, and sells 180 million cups of coffee every year.

In total, EG Group employs about 33,000 people and operates roughly 4,300 sites. In recent years, EG has substantially reduced its borrowings by selling assets, including offloading the majority of its U.K. sites to Asda. The group has also sold its operations in Italy, and is close to doing so in Australia.

Healthy IPO Action

EG Group is roughly 50% owned by TDR Capital, the London-based private equity firm, which also owns a controlling stake in U.K. supermarket Asda. The brothers own 25% of the company each.

The U.S. is its biggest single market, and as such its global headquarters are moving to Charlotte in North Carolina. Its chief executive, Russell Colaco, is also based in the U.S.

It marks a healthy start to 2026 for U.S. IPO announcements as pent-up demand following the U.S. government shutdown last year bursts onto the market. Bitcoin miner BitGo (BTGO) and construction group EquipmentShare (EQPT) have already gone public. Other big names which might list this year include Anthropic and OpenAI.

However, the decision last week by brokerage Clear Street to postpone its IPO citing market conditions could cause others to think twice. Analysts said the Clear Street decision was down to a slump in crypto trading and the recent AI-driven selloff in financial and software stocks.

What Other IPOs are in the Pipeline?

Let's look at other IPOs in the pipeline with our TipRanks IPO calendar.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Apple Stock Forecast — Should You Catch this Falling Knife?
data.description:
Apple (AAPL) shares have dropped nearly 8% over the past five days after the company delayed its highly anticipated Siri AI upgrades. Investors are wary of repeated Siri setbacks and Apple's late AI entry.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

A Bloomberg report noted that Apple's Siri update has hit testing snags, potentially delaying several features. Apple originally targeted iOS 26.4 for the March release but now plans phased rollouts across multiple updates. Some are expected with iOS 26.5 in May or even with iOS 27 in September.

Here's What Analysts Say

Evercore ISI analyst Amit Daryanani maintained his Buy rating and $330 price target (29% upside potential) despite news of the delay. The five-star analyst noted that testing issues have forced Siri to stick with its existing ChatGPT integration rather than Apple's new tech. He expects gradual feature rollouts starting mid-year.

iOS 26.5 will introduce a new web search tool and custom image generation. Additionally, iOS 27 brings a major AI overhaul, possibly integrating Google's (GOOGL) Gemini models. Daryanani also highlighted that Apple's Q1FY26 results beat expectations on sales and earnings, boosted by strong Services growth and low memory chip costs.

For the March quarter, Apple had guided for 13%-16% revenue growth, above prior estimates, backed by solid iPhone supply and continued Services momentum. The company is focusing on data privacy and smooth integration for its upcoming AI features.

Similarly, Robert W. Baird analyst William Power also reiterated his Buy rating and $300 price target, suggesting 17.3% upside potential. His price target suggests a 35x 2026 EPS forecast, sitting at the high end of Apple's post-COVID range (25-35x). AAPL stock valuation is also above peers in tech/consumer staples, but is justified by solid execution, rising Services revenue, ecosystem strength, and lower capital needs.

Meanwhile, Power sees risks such as component pricing and supply chain issues, regulatory and geopolitical pressures, competition from Google, Samsung (SSNLF), Microsoft (MSFT) as well as macroeconomic headwinds, changing consumer tastes, and high valuation.

Is AAPL a Good Stock to Buy?

Despite Siri delays causing an 8% five-day AAPL drop, analysts like Daryanani and Power maintain Buy ratings, viewing it as a calculated risk for long-term investors given strong Services growth and phased AI rollouts.

On TipRanks, Apple stock has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 17 Buys, nine Holds, and one Sell rating. The average Apple price target of $307.55 implies 20.2% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, AAPL shares have gained 4.6%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Analysts See Further Upside in Vertiv Stock (VRT) after Stellar Earnings
data.description:
While software stocks like Salesforce (CRM), ServiceNow (NOW), and Microsoft (MSFT) are being battered amid concerns about potential artificial intelligence (AI)-led disruption, shares of Vertiv Holdings (VRT) have rallied about 45% year to date, driven by robust demand for data center infrastructure. The provider of power and cooling solutions delivered impressive fourth-quarter results last week, with organic orders growing 252% year over year. Given a strong demand backdrop, analysts remain bullish on the AI infrastructure stock and see further upside potential.  

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Analysts Raise Price Targets for Vertiv Stock

In reaction to the impressive Q4 print, TD Cowen analyst Michael Elias raised the price target for Vertiv stock to $269 from $211 and reiterated a Buy rating. The 4-star analyst noted that Vertiv reported record Q4 2025 implied order volume of $8.4 billion, up from $3.7 billion in Q3 2025 and $2.3 billion in Q4 2024. Noting solid data center-led demand in the U.S., Elias continues to see upside for Vertiv through 2026, with his firm's checks indicating continued "positive price-cost," enhanced margins, and solid near-term order volumes, provided the company continues to enhance its capacity.

Elias views VRT as his "Best Idea" for 2026 based on the robust demand backdrop. The analyst highlighted that despite the large orders in Q4 2025, management expects continued growth, driven mainly by the demand in the Americas.

Likewise, Goldman Sachs analyst Mark Delaney raised the price target for Vertiv stock to $277 from $204 and reiterated a Buy rating following the impressive Q4 results. The 5-star analyst noted that Vertiv continues to be well-positioned for long-term growth tied to data center infrastructure, as reflected in solid Q4 orders.

Also, Morgan Stanley analyst Christopher Snyder boosted his price target to $285 from $200 and reaffirmed a Buy rating. The 4-star analyst is confident about the "durability" of Vertiv's revenue growth, innovation, and "inflecting" service opportunity.

Is VRT a Good Stock to Buy?

Overall, Wall Street is highly bullish on Vertiv Holdings stock, with a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 14 Buys and one Hold recommendation. The average VRT stock price target of $275 indicates 17.3% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Chevron Moves into Greek Waters with New Gas Exploration Agreements
data.description:
Chevron (CVX) is expanding its energy footprint in the eastern Mediterranean after a consortium led by the U.S. oil major signed exclusive agreements to explore for natural gas off southern Greece. The deal expands Greece's offshore exploration area and is the second recent agreement with a U.S. energy company, reflecting Europe's efforts to move away from Russian supplies and increase U.S. energy involvement.

For context, Chevron is one of the world's largest integrated energy companies, operating across oil, gas, and renewable energy.

data.title: Options Volatility and Implied Earnings Moves This Week, February 16 – February 19, 2026
data.description:
This week, several major companies will be reporting earnings. Among them are Walmart (WMT), Energy Transfer (ET), Palo Alto Networks (PANW), Occidental Petroleum (OXY), CoreWeave (CRWV).

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ahead of this week's major earnings announcements, TipRanks has calculated the expected moves of these companies' stocks. We use the at-the-money straddle of the options whose post-earnings expiration dates are closest to the earnings announcement date.

Note that options volatility tends to be high in the days leading up to an earnings announcement, due to uncertainty around the earnings. Investors don't know whether the earnings will be positive or negative, so the price of options (aka the option premium) rises.

Furthermore, after the earnings announcement, because the uncertainty has been resolved, the price of options (the option premium) decreases substantially. This is known as the volatility crush.

data.title: Alibaba (BABA) Upgrades Qwen AI Model as China's Tech Giants Battle for Users
data.description:
Alibaba (BABA) refreshed its flagship AI model with a new Qwen3.5 upgrade, stepping up its push in China's rapidly developing artificial intelligence space.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The updated model can understand text, images, and video and is built to handle "AI agent" tasks, allowing it to act on a user's behalf. It can also review videos up to two hours long, supporting uses such as research, shopping assistance, and automated services.

AI Competition Intensifies in China

The release comes just as China's AI sector prepares for another wave of product launches. Rivals including Baidu (BIDU) and Tencent Holdings (TCEHY), along with several startups, have recently rolled out upgrades of their own. The companies are also preparing for a widely expected new platform from DeepSeek.

The timing also matters because the Lunar New Year holiday brings a surge in app usage across China, making it an important period for testing whether new services can attract and retain users.

Competition is also moving beyond technology. Alibaba, Baidu, and Tencent are offering cash rewards and promotions to draw users to their chatbots and secure market share early while adoption is still developing.

Alibaba Expands Its AI Strategy

Alibaba has been increasing its focus on AI since last year's surge in interest in AI models. The company plans to spend tens of billions of dollars on cloud systems and AI tools, seeing the technology as a key growth area beyond online shopping.

The Qwen app is also expanding into a broader service platform linked to Alibaba's ecosystem. Last month, Alibaba upgraded Qwen so users can order food delivery, book travel, and make payments directly within the chat. Users can now handle everyday tasks inside the app through its AI assistants. 

Is Alibaba Stock a Good Buy Right Now?   

Analysts remain highly bullish about Alibaba's stock trajectory. With 15 unanimous Buy ratings, BABA stock commands a Strong Buy consensus rating on TipRanks. Also, the average Alibaba price target of $203.09 implies about 30.41% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: X Prepares to Launch In-App Trading, which Elon Musk Says Is 'Going to Be a Game-Changer'
data.description:
Elon Musk is ready to execute on his plan to turn X into the world's biggest financial platform. X's head of product, Nikita Bier, confirmed that the platform will launch a feature called "Smart Cashtags" in just a few weeks. This new tool will allow users to buy and sell stocks and cryptocurrencies without ever leaving their feed. Musk has high hopes for the new system, recently telling employees during an internal meeting: "This is really intended to be the place where all the money is. It's really going to be a game-changer.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Elon Musk Wants to Facilitate Trading Directly on X

The new system is a massive upgrade from the old, static ticker symbols. Under the new plan, when a user sees a tag like $BTC or $TSLA in a post, they can tap it to see live price charts and execute trades instantly. It's important to note that X isn't trying to become a traditional stockbroker itself; instead, the platform will act as a front door that connects users to existing trading partners and exchanges. The goal is to make investing as easy as liking a post. Musk believes that by removing the friction of switching between different apps, X can capture a huge portion of the hundreds of billions of dollars moved by financial news every day.

The Trading Launch Is Part of X Money

This trading launch is the centerpiece of X Money, the platform's new payments arm. Musk revealed that the system has already finished internal testing with employees and will open to a limited group of external users within the next month or two. Backed by a partnership with Visa (V), X Money will handle everything from peer-to-peer payments, similar to Venmo, to settling global transactions. Musk's vision is to mirror the success of super apps like China's WeChat, where one single application handles your social life, your shopping, and your entire bank account.

Musk's Plan Has Sparked Security Debates

While the plan is ambitious, it has sparked a lot of debate among security experts. Some worry that making it "too easy" to buy assets like Dogecoin could lead to risky, impulsive decisions. To deal with this, X is updating its rules to shut down spammy apps and scam accounts that try to trick people into fake fee pools or raids. Musk's head of product, Nikita Bier, recently said that while they want crypto to "proliferate" on the site, they are strictly blocking any apps that harass users or create unfair incentives for scammers.

Investors can track Elon Musk and his ventures on TipRanks. Click on the image below to find out more.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: China Ready to Dazzle the World with Humanoid Robots 'Super Bowl' Show
data.description:
Anyone who thought the Super Bowl had been and gone needs to tune into Chinese television this evening where China is ready to show off its humanoid robotics leadership to the world and President Xi.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

According to a Reuters article, humanoid robots will be the star of China's most-watched TV show, the annual CCTV Spring Festival gala. It is comparable in its size and popularity to the Super Bowl in the U.S.

Gala of Innovations

Four humanoid startups – Unitree Robotics, Galbot, Noetix and MagicLab – are set to show off their robotic innovations at the gala. It will take some going to beat last year's event which apparently included 16 full-size Unitree humanoids twirling handkerchiefs and dancing in unison with human – remember them? – performers.

The CCTV show, which drew 79% of live TV viewership in China last year, has for decades been used to highlight Beijing's tech ambitions including its space programme, drones and robotics, ⁠Georg Stieler, Asia managing director and head of robotics and automation at technology consultancy Stieler, told Reuters.

"What distinguishes the gala from comparable events elsewhere is the directness of the pipeline from industrial policy to prime-time spectacle," Stieler said. "Companies that appear on the gala stage receive tangible rewards in government orders, investor attention, and market access."
data.author: David Craik
